'''Salesi Finau''' (born 5 May 1973)<ref name="prem">{{cite web |url=http://www.espnscrum.com/blogs/rugby/player/12975.html |title=Salesi Finau |publisher=ESPNscrum |accessdate=28 December 2020}}</ref> is a Tongan former rugby footballer. He represented Tonga in both the [[rugby league]] and [[rugby union]] sides.

==Background==
Finau in [[Neiafu (Vava'u)|Neiafu]], [[Vava'u]], [[Tonga]].

==Rugby league career==
Salesi began his career with the [[Canberra Raiders]],<ref>{{cite web |url=https://www.warringtonguardian.co.uk/sport/18951247.tongas-salesi-finau-mateaki-mafi-warrington-wolves/ |title=Tonga's Salesi Finau and Mateaki Mafi at Warrington Wolves |author=Mike Parsons |publisher=Warrington Guardian |access-date=8 October 2021}}</ref> and played in the [[Tonga national rugby league team]] at the 1995 World Cup.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/tonga-robbed-by-ridges-late-kicks-1576808.html|first=Dave|last=Hadfield|date=9 October 1995|title=Tonga robbed by Ridge's late kicks| location=London| work=The Independent}}</ref> After impressing during the tournament he signed for [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]],<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/tongans-head-for-warrington-1584121.html|date=28 November 1995|first=Dave|last=Hadfield|title=Tongans head for Warrington| location=London| work=The Independent}}</ref>

==Rugby union career==
===Club===
In late 1998 Finau signed for Welsh rugby union side [[Scarlets|Llanelli Scarlets]] under coach Gareth Jenkins.<ref>{{Cite news|date=12 November 1998|first=David|last=Llewelyn|url=https://www.independent.co.uk/sport/rugby-union-springboks-cite-flanker-white-1184419.html|title=Rugby union: Springboks cite flanker White| location=London| work=The Independent}}</ref> Finau made 203 appearances for the side over the next six-year, becoming an instrumental team player, playing in 3 European Cup semi-finals and 2 Celtic League Titles, before ending his contract by mutual consent in September 2005.<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport1/hi/rugby_union/welsh/4217340.stm|title=Scarlets exit for homebound Finau|date=5 September 2005|publisher=BBC News }}</ref> He then signed for [[Bath Rugby]] under [[John Connolly (rugby)|John Connolly]],<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_union/english/4225268.stm|publisher=BBC News|title=Bath sign up Scarlets wing Finau|date=8 September 2005 }}</ref> spending a season at the West Country club before moving to French side [[CS Bourgoin-Jallieu|Bourgoin]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.rugby365.fr/top-14/article_126178_Bourgoin-Presentation-des-recrues.shtml|title=Bourgoin: Présentation des recrues|language=French|publisher=Rugby365.fr|date=18 July 2006|archive-date=21 July 2011|access-date=28 February 2010|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20110721020758/http://www.rugby365.fr/top-14/article_126178_Bourgoin-Presentation-des-recrues.shtml|url-status=dead}}</ref> Finau had a successful season at Bourgoin, and on completion announced that he had signed a 2-year contract for [[Cornish Pirates]].<ref>{{Cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport2/hi/rugby_union/6571719.stm|title=Pirates sign ex-Bath player Finau|date=19 April 2007|publisher=BBC News }}</ref> However, in June 2007, the club announced that they had agreed to release Finau from the deal when a family illness obliged him to return to his native Tonga.<ref>{{Cite news|date=28 June 2007|publisher=Exeter Express and Echo|url=http://www.highbeam.com/doc/1P2-14534291.html|title= Pirates release Finau from deal on family grounds }}{{dead link|date=February 2019|bot=medic}}{{cbignore|bot=medic}}</ref>

===Country===
Finau received at least 13 caps for the Tongan national rugby union side,<ref name="prem"/> and played in the Tongan [[rugby sevens]] side at the [[2002 Commonwealth Games]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://news.bbc.co.uk/sport3/commonwealthgames2002/bsp/statistics/events/rugby_results.stm|publisher=BBC|title=Statistics: rugby sevens }}</ref>

==TV career==

Salesi currently presents a cooking show on Television Tonga called 'Salsa with Salesi'.<ref>{{Cite news|url=http://www.tonga-broadcasting.net/?page_id=22|title= Tonga Broadcasting TV Programs}}</ref>
